item.title: 349 more Filipinos repatriated from Middle East
item.description: Another batch of overseas Filipino workers (OFWs) and stranded individuals arrived at Villamor Air Base, Pasay City on Friday evening aboard a chartered flight amid the ongoing crisis in the Middle East.

item.title: Marcos: ‘Bare-boned’ ASEAN Summit in May to focus on oil, food, migrant workers
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. said Friday that the 48th ASEAN Summit and Related Summits in May would push through under a “very bare-boned” setup as part of the government’s cost-cutting measures to cope with the national energy emergency brought about by the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Lacson flags Marcoleta for comparing oil companies' profits to ‘maleta deliveries’
item.description: Senate President Pro Tempore and Senate Blue Ribbon Committee Chair Panfilo Lacson flags the analogy of Senator Rodante Marcoleta of oil companies allegedly profiting from expensive fuel and the alleged cash deliveries made by alleged former soldiers to government officials.

item.title: Pasig court to inspect KOJC properties in Metro Manila, Davao City
item.description: The Pasig City Regional Trial Court Branch 159 granted the motion of the prosecution to conduct ocular inspections of properties associated with Kingdom of Jesus Christ leader Pastor Apollo Quiboloy.

item.title: Fugitive Atong Ang still in PH, BI spokesperson says
item.description: Fugitive businessman Atong Ang, who is accused of masterminding the disappearance of several cockfight enthusiasts or sabungeros, is still in the Philippines, Bureau of Immigration on Friday.

item.title: Rhian Ramos to pursue possible legal action over driver’s assault claims
item.description: Actress Rhian Ramos said she is already looking into possible legal actions against her former driver, who alleged that she, Michelle Dee, and Sam Panlilio physically assaulted and illegally detained him back in January.

item.title: ‘I am deeply sorry’: Suntay’s wife breaks silence on husband's Anne Curtis remark
item.description: The wife of Rep. Bong Suntay has issued a public statement distancing herself and her children from the controversial remarks the Quezon City lawmaker made during a House impeachment hearing, after his comments referencing actress Anne Curtis sparked widespread backlash online.

item.title: No double standard in impeachment proceedings — House justice panel members
item.description: Leaders of the House Committee on Justice asserted there was no double standard in its deliberations on the impeachment complaints against the country’s two highest officials.

item.title: DSWD urges stronger cooperation among ASEAN states amid global tensions
item.description: Social Welfare Secretary Rex Gatchalian called on other members of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) for stronger cooperation amid global headwinds.

item.title: Man wanted for inciting to sedition surrenders to police
item.description: The Quezon City Police District on Thursday said it has successfully served a warrant of arrest against an individual wanted for inciting to sedition.
